method_atom: A construction technique for globally regular solutions of a nonlinear geometric field theory by reducing along a single continuous (rotational) symmetry direction over a lower-dimensional base manifold whose metric signature reverses across a critical hypersurface, combined with divergent conformal/warp factors tuned so that apparent singularities of the base and the warp factors cancel and are replaced by smooth, topologically nontrivial cycles (a 'bubbling' resolution). The same reduction-plus-warping machinery can also deform one regular background into a different one carrying an enhanced boundary symmetry.
object_atom: Five-dimensional, three-charge supersymmetric supergravity solutions with only a U(1) isometry, built over hyper-Kahler base spaces (ambi-polar generalizations of the Atiyah-Hitchin and Eguchi-Hanson metrics), including a bubbled global AdS_2 x S^3 compactification.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the signature-changing-base plus singular-warp-factor reduction that yields a globally smooth higher-dimensional solution with topological bubbles; the supergravity fields, charges, and specific hyper-Kahler base metrics are the domain-specific object.
